headlines.title: ENGINEERSORDERED TO REIMBURSE CITY
headlines.subtitle: Commission Resolution Asks That Lake Worth Losses Be Made Good.
headlines.fullText:
J. D. Trammell, John B. Hawley and T. U. Taylor, comprising the original board of reservoir engineers, will be held to account to the city for any losses sustained through alleged over- estimates of work on Lake Worth. A demand was made in resolutions passed by the city commission Saturday that the word and a failure to do so City Attorney MeCart is Instruct- ed to enter suit.
The resolutions do not name the en- peers included order bet Com tineers thelmded in the order, but Com- city Attorney Se ha a affect Trammell. Hawley and Taylor. The alleged gyere antalet during their administration and before W. B. King was connected with the engineering board.
A copy of resolution will be served Trammell. Hawley and Taylor by City Secretary Harwood by registered
Contractors Reports Sought.
C. T. Hodge, who cleared reservoir Fund: Dennis Fagan and the Hedges to file reports with the commission Showing the extent of work completed under their contracts with the elty. Commissioner Blanke made a motion asking for such reports.
J. D. Trammell, chairman of the board of engineers, asked that the probe into the condition of the dam base at the reservoir be continued. In a com- munication to the commission Chair- man Trammell said the Chamber of Commerce advisory committee had made serious charges relative to this matter and he wanted a thorough in- Fertigation. Superintendent Lord asket for permission to fill up the tunnels Hade necessary to conquer the lives fications commandent ahora that the superinternet De ,thetrueten to fill the excavations and proceed with construction work.
Want Conduit Completed.
A formal demand upon the Hedges .A Termo deman made in solution asking for the completion of the conduit line and the placing of the already constructed work in proper con- dition to satisfy specifications. Ten days was given the company to notify the city as to probable action to be siren and thirty days was given as the limit in which to begin actual work. The resolution demanded that the present work be completed at once. The conduit line laying in the bottom of Lake Worth from the dam to the intake stand will be the first piece of new work to be taken up upon the resumption of Work.

Paving notes amounting to $7,500 and water department bills to the extent of $1.200 were ordered paid.

headlines.title: MINERS ASKED TO PASS RESOLUTION RAPPING AMMONS
headlines.fullText:
By Asmeisted Press, INDIANAPOLIS, Jan. 24-Governor E. M. Ammons of Colorado is con- demned in a resolution presented to the convention of the United Mine Workers of America by the union at Oak Creek, Colo, and which was be- tore convention today for action.
Torp the coragem de the deport- ation of Charles H. Moyer, president of the Western Federation of Miners. to Wemmer trike zone in 3tlchi- man and extends "our deepest wym- pathy to our brothers and their fam- ities in Calumet, on account of their HeesChristmas Eve, which we believe was caused by the mine owners and their hired thugs and as- One resolution requires the conven- tion to call on congress to pass a law making it unlawful for any person, firm or corporation to import into a strike district from any other state any strikebreakers, nonunion workmet. do- tertives or gunmen for the purpose of breaking strikes, it also pellets law to prohibit the use of mate troops in strike districts.

headlines.title: NEGRO LOST LIFE IN THURSDAY FIRE; NO REMAINS FOUND
headlines.fullText:
Cam Smith, 68, negro, employed by the Trinity Compress Company, was burned to death in the fire that de- troyed the compress Thursday. Smith lived at Eleventh and Stedman streets with a negro boarding house actuele Gussle Willis, It was not known unti late Friday that the negro was miss
Smith, a cripple, during the fire was reen on the cotton Dialtern belleved he became Trapped in town flames. Firemen have been unable
Gussie Willis maid Smith had not bees home since the fire. At noon Saturday the cotton was still burning.

headlines.title: CONTRACTOR TELLS OF FIRING ON BANDITS
headlines.fullText:
MEMPHIS, Jan. 24-Conductor J. LA. Armstrong of the sound train held up last night, told the for" lowing story of the robbery on his ar- "When the train was stopped 1 gineer had stopped to put off some grampe, but walked forward through the train to the baggage car. When I reached It. the harwareman. W. A. Campbell, told the not to stick my head out, that someone had ordered him to keep inside or be killed.
I grabbed my pistol and walked to called to me to get back, and added a fout name. I shot toward the flash- light and an answering shot was re- turned. In all four shots were fired. The robbers compelled the fireman to uncouple the express cat. Tlien they ordered the engineer, fireman and ex- press messenger back to the baggage car and ran the express car unear paris forward, Here they Die Welt === == ran away. The express messenger said there was nothing in his safe to reward them.
"It was too dark to see anything but in number, finally realized Ithat the train was being held up, but I they behaved well and there Was no benven was excitement, even on the part of The hold-up men to attack anything but the express car."

headlines.title: AMERICAN TROOPS FIRED ON FROM ACROSS RIO GRANDE
headlines.fullText: Ily Assorintet Prest, ALPINE, Texas, Jan. : 4 .- American soldiers at Lafitas, Texas, were fired on from the Mexican alde of the Rio Grande yesterday, according to a re- port received here today. No one was injured. It was at La- jitas that Major Gonzales, a constitu- tionalist leader, was arrested recently by state authorities on a charge of car- Trying a pistol.

headlines.title: HANGER OUT OF GOVERNOR'S RACE; WILL TELL WHY
headlines.fullText:
W. A. Hanger of Fort Worth has withdrawn from the gubernatorial race. He will make his formal an- bouncement to that effect in the Sun- day morning newspapers- This was rumored a week ago tt Hanger then refused to commit him- self. He was asked again regarding the rumer hallintay. "I will announce my withdrawal Sunday morning "
have been intending to do this for two weeks but I have simply been too busy to get to It. Hanger refused to give his reasons explain that in his formal announce- ment

headlines.title: LITTLE INTEREST IN BEAUMONT POLITICS

headlines.fullText: BEAUMONT, Jan. 24. headlines.title: BANK ROBBERS BLOW SAFE FOR $5,000.
headlines.fullText: LUDLOW, Mo. Jan. 24.Rob= bers blew open the vault in the Farmers Bank here early today ad escaped with $5,000, mostly silver.

headlines.title: Mrs. Wilson Endows Scholarship.
headlines.fullText: ROSTE G8. Jan. 24-Endowment by Mes Woodrow Wilson of the Etward W Axson scholarship in the Martha Merry School for Mountain Girls here announced yesterday at the cele- beation of that institution's twelfth an- niversary.
